rule_summary: Study-specific compiler rules for Gill 2022 sorted-bulk maturation-phase conditions. These rules preserve the stronger temporal structure and the successful-versus-failing branch distinction without pretending the family is single-cell.

conditions.condition_summary: Gill successful maturation branch with real later-phase timing and relatively favorable proxy identity profile.
conditions.rule_basis: Treat the successful sorted fraction as the strongest current maturation-phase positive family, while still preserving bulk-limited uncertainty through the supporting evidence class.

conditions.condition_summary: Gill failing fraction in the maturation window; negative pressure case inside the same family.
conditions.rule_basis: Treat the failing sorted fraction as the strongest current maturation-phase negative family within the same study context as the positive-looking branch.

conditions.condition_summary: Gill maturation condition with age-linked improvement but nontrivial identity pressure that should resist clean promotion.
source_locators: Intermediate-pressure boundary case between successful and failing branches
conditions.rule_basis: Preserve a medium-pressure maturation condition so the benchmark can demonstrate downgrade versus abstention behavior between the clearly favorable and clearly failing Gill branches.
